title: module parameter, procedural assignment to wire detection is broken
body:
The following should not be valid Verilog.
```verilog
module bug
	(input clk,
	output reg reg1,
	output reg2);
always @(posedge clk) begin
	reg1 <= ~reg1;
	reg2 <= ~reg2;
end
endmodule
```
This isn't valid as there is a procedural assignment to the wire reg2.
By adding reg to the first occurrence of this issue in the module's params, all subsequent are considered valid. 
If the second or third is correct all preceding incorrect are detected, but all after are valid.

This happens on Verilator 5.009 devel on Debian Sid
